title: Deterministic Card Rendering
source: Current Codex thread on skill execution stability

question_answered: How can a learning card keep a stable final structure instead of relying on ad hoc manual formatting every time?
one_sentence_definition: Deterministic Card Rendering means deciding the content first and then using a fixed renderer to produce the final card structure.
essence: It separates semantic judgment from structural output so each layer can become stable in its own way.
why_it_matters: It reduces formatting drift across runs.
It makes skill outputs easier to compare, validate, and maintain.
core_logic: Extract the concept-level content from the thread.
Map that content into fixed sections.
Let a renderer generate the final Markdown shape.
prerequisites: The target card structure is already stable enough to render deterministically.
The input has already been narrowed to one clear concept.
failure_boundaries: If the input still mixes multiple concepts, deterministic rendering will only produce consistently mixed output.
If the schema keeps changing, renderer stability will also drift.
confusions: It is easy to confuse this with simply having a template file; the key difference is a fixed rendering flow.
examples: First build a JSON spec, then run the renderer to produce the final card.
counter_examples: Manually copying an old card and editing it freehand until field order and section style slowly diverge.
common_misunderstandings: A stable structure does not automatically mean high-quality content.
my_words: Let me think about the content, and let the renderer own the shape.
needs_validation: When updating an existing card, should the same rendering skeleton still be reused whenever possible?

parent_concepts: [[Skill Output Stability]] | Deterministic rendering is a lower-level implementation of output stability.
child_concepts: [[Deterministic Method Rendering]] | Method-card rendering is one concrete branch of this idea.
adjacent_concepts: [[Template-Driven Writing]] | Both pursue consistency, but one relies on a renderer and the other leans on a static template.

prerequisites: [[Single Concept Capture]] | Deterministic rendering only helps after the card boundary is already compressed to one concept.
enables: [[Prompt Narrowing]] | Stable rendering makes it easier to compare outputs from narrowed prompts.
contrasts: [[Template-Driven Writing]] | One emphasizes a fixed generation flow, the other emphasizes a reusable template.
corrections: [[Templates Alone Guarantee Stability]] | This concept corrects the mistaken idea that templates alone are sufficient.

direct_routes: When the thread is still defining what deterministic rendering is, stay in this concept card before switching to implementation details.
If the idea is clear but the next question becomes how to apply it in a workflow, route to [[Prompt Narrowing]].
secondary_routes: If [[Prompt Narrowing]] clarifies task scope but renderer behavior is still unstable, continue to [[Deterministic Method Rendering]].
gap_signals: If the graph contains templates and renderers but no card explains output stability at a higher level, add a new Concept card for that parent abstraction.
stop_rules: Stop staying in this concept once the main question changes from definition to execution design.

current_status_notes: This concept already has reliable local graph placement and early routing value.
next_goal: Add a stronger arbitration rule for when to move from definition work to method or mechanism work.
growing_checklist: Explain why semantic generation and structural rendering should be separated.
stable_checklist: Provide at least three strong node-level relationships.
Explain where this concept sits inside the local knowledge graph.
expert_ready_checklist: Add stronger multi-hop routes from concept clarification into method or mechanism execution.
Add at least one explicit gap signal and one stop rule.

main_reasons: The concept already works as a reliable local node.
It has early direct routes but not enough dispatch depth for expert-ready.
missing_evidence: It still lacks stronger cross-card arbitration rules.
Its gap-detection behavior is still narrow.
next_rules: When definition work turns into workflow design, route to a method card.
When definition work turns into causal explanation, route to a mechanism card.

current_upgrade_tasks: Add one repeated real-world example where deterministic rendering prevented structural drift.
upgrade_history: 2026-03-27: migrated routing to the four-part dispatch structure
